summary: Comprehensive frontend testing of Nova AI Pricing page (/pricing). All 55 test assertions passed across structure, content, pricing logic (monthly/annual), CTAs, features, navigation, theme toggle, and mobile responsiveness.
critical_code_review_comments: Pricing.jsx is well-structured at ~324 lines with clean separation: TIERS data, BillingToggle, PricingCard, Pricing default export.

All required data-testid attributes are present (pricing-page, billing-toggle, billing-toggle-btn, pricing-card-{tier}, popular-badge, tier-name-{tier}, tier-price-{cadet|operator}, cta-{tier}, feature-{tier}-{0-3}).
Annual math correctly implemented with Math.round(monthlyPrice * 0.8) — Cadet $19→$15, Operator $99→$79.
Operator card has gradient-shift animated border + purple glow boxShadow + scale-105; MOST POPULAR badge positioned correctly.
Strikethrough annual totals shown: Cadet $180/yr vs $228/yr crossed; Operator $948/yr vs $1188/yr crossed.
Recruit stays $0 and Command stays 'Custom' in both billing modes — verified.
Trust bar shows all three badges (SSL, SOC 2, 99.9% Uptime SLA).
Mobile (390px): cards stack vertically with full width (~342px), confirmed via bounding box positions.

Minor nit: in the non-primary CTA branch, the ternary `tier.monthlyPrice === null ? '#' : tier.monthlyPrice === 0 ? '/login' : '/login'` collapses to '/login' for both — could be simplified, but no functional impact.
Theme toggle works correctly on /pricing (light→dark transition verified via data-theme attribute change).
test_credentials: Not required — pricing page is public.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pricing page fully verified at /pricing. All 14 acceptance criteria from the review request passed: heading, 4 cards, prices, CTAs, features, popular badge, monthly/annual toggle math, annual save-20% badge, strikethrough totals, Recruit/Command invariants, theme toggle, navbar link, trust bar, and mobile stacking. No issues found.
